name: Silence & Contract Monitor - Backstory Starter
description: Monitors accounts for engagement gaps that may signal churn risk. Every morning at 6:30 AM, the workflow pulls accounts and checks for those that have "gone silent" — no meaningful engagement activity within a configured lookback window. For flagged accounts, it uses the LLM to assess the severity of the silence, considering deal stage, contract dates, and historical patterns. Accounts deemed concerning are surfaced via Alert (Slack, Teams, or Email) so the owning rep or CSM can re-engage.

nodes.name: Workflow Parameters
nodes.notes: Fetch customer accounts, their renewal/contract dates, and the last meaningful engagement timestamp. Replace the placeholder API endpoints and tokens with real systems before enabling this workflow.

nodes.name: Get Backstory Auth Token
nodes.notes: Optional if you are using MCP only, but kept here to match the existing repo’s n8n patterns.

nodes.name: Fetch Source Records
nodes.notes: Fetch customer accounts, their renewal/contract dates, and the last meaningful engagement timestamp.

nodes.name: Normalize Source Records
nodes.notes: Converts raw silent accounts responses into one item per record for downstream AI analysis.

nodes.name: Analyze Silence & Contract Monitor

parameters.text:
=You are generating the Silence & Contract Monitor workflow output.

PURPOSE:
Monitors accounts for engagement gaps that may signal churn risk. Every morning at 6:30 AM, the workflow pulls accounts and checks for those that have "gone silent" — no meaningful engagement activity within a configured lookback window. For flagged accounts, it uses the LLM to assess the severity of the silence, considering deal stage, contract dates, and historical patterns. Accounts deemed concerning are surfaced via Alert (Slack, Teams, or Email) so the owning rep or CSM can re-engage.

SOURCE PAYLOAD:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.record, null, 2) }}

TASKS:
1. Use Backstory MCP to enrich the record with the most relevant account, opportunity, engagement, or stakeholder context.
2. Decide what matters most for the silence & contract monitor use case.
3. Produce a concise markdown summary with: headline, key findings, risks, recommended actions, and routing notes if applicable.

OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS:
- Keep it compact enough for Slack.
- Prefer bullets.
- Mention concrete dates, owners, amounts, or stages when the data provides them.
- If the source record is incomplete, explicitly say what is missing.

REFERENCE STYLE:
🔴 **SILENCE ALERT** — 3 accounts require immediate attention

**GLOBEX INDUSTRIES** | ===$340,000=== | Renewal: 04/2026 | 🔴 Critical
- Last meaningful engagement: **18 days ago** (normally 3-day cadence)
- Champion @lisa.wong has not opened last 4 emails
- Contract renewal in 47 days — no renewal discussion initiated
- 👉 @david.kim: Schedule a check-in call citing Q2 planning as reason to reconnect

**INITECH** | ===$125,000=== | Renewal: 06/2026 | 🟡 Watch
- Last engagement: **11 days ago** — below 7-day norm

options.systemMessage: You are a Backstory workflow analyst generating Silence & Contract Monitor outputs. Keep the response actionable, concrete, and Slack-friendly.

nodes.notes: Wire your preferred LLM credentials to the chat model node after import.

nodes.name: Backstory MCP
nodes.notes: This mirrors the existing workflow infrastructure in the repo. Add MCP auth headers after import.

nodes.name: Aggregate Results
nodes.notes: Collapses all analyzed records into a single outbound summary. Replace this with per-owner routing if you want one message per rep/CSM/executive.

nodes.name: Post to Slack
nodes.notes: Swap this node for Teams or SMTP if Slack is not your delivery target.
